Naked Wines PLC

12 June 2026
Naked Wines plc
('Naked Wines' or 'Group')
Transaction in Own Shares and Completion of Share Buyback Programme
Naked Wines announces that, in accordance with the terms of its share buyback programme announced on 24 March 2026 ("
Share Buyback Programme
"), the Company purchased the following number of its ordinary shares of 7.5 pence each ("
Ordinary Shares
") through Panmure Liberum Limited in the period from 08 June 2026 to 11 June 2026.
Date of Purchase
Number of ordinary shares purchased

Following the purchase of these shares, the remaining number of Ordinary Shares in issue will be 71,717,079 with 5,460,795 shares held in treasury. The total voting rights in the Company will therefore be 66,256,284. This figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 (the Market Abuse Regulation), as it forms part of Retained EU Law as defined in the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018, the table below contains detailed information of the individual trades made by Panmure Liberum Limited as part of the Share Buyback Programme.
Following the above purchase of shares, the Company announces that it has now completed its Share Buyback Programme, to acquire up to a maximum aggregate consideration of £1 million.
In total, the Group repurchased 1,355,130 ordinary shares at an average price of 73.79 pence, for a total consideration of £999,999.

About Naked Wines
Naked Wines
is not just an online wine retailer; we're trailblazers on a mission to enable enthusiastic wine drinkers to enjoy great wine without the guesswork.Founded in 2008, on the pillars of quality, choice and fair pricing, we set out to create the most inclusive wine club in the world - dedicated to transforming the wine-buying experience and empowering people to make their own wine choices, and championing world-class independent winemakers. We've proudly been delivering outstanding wines to our customers (who we call Angels) for over 16 years.
Our business model is simple yet innovative: Naked Wines funds the production costs for winemakers upfront, allowing them to focus on creating exceptional wines without the financial burdens of traditional wine production, while passing the resulting savings back to our customers.
The virtuous circle is a win-win for both wine lovers and winemakers, and enables us to deliver superior benefits to our customers:
- Better quality wine
- More choice
- Personalised wine recommendations
- Elimination of guesswork and uncertainty
- Fair payments for all involved
Our Angel customers in the UK, US and Australia have direct access to over 300 world-class independent winemakers and over 2,500 quality wines from 23 countries.
For more information visit
nakedwinesplc.co.uk
and
nakedwines.co.uk
or find us
@nakedwines
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. About Naked Wines
Naked Wines
is not just an online wine retailer; we're trailblazers on a mission to enable enthusiastic wine drinkers to enjoy great wine without the guesswork.Founded in 2008, on the pillars of quality, choice and fair pricing, we set out to create the most inclusive wine club in the world - dedicated to transforming the wine-buying experience and empowering people to make their own wine choices, and championing world-class independent winemakers. We've proudly been delivering outstanding wines to our customers (who we call Angels) for over 16 years.
Our business model is simple yet innovative: Naked Wines funds the production costs for winemakers upfront, allowing them to focus on creating exceptional wines without the financial burdens of traditional wine production, while passing the resulting savings back to our customers.
The virtuous circle is a win-win for both wine lovers and winemakers, and enables us to deliver superior benefits to our customers:
- Better quality wine
- More choice
- Personalised wine recommendations
- Elimination of guesswork and uncertainty
- Fair payments for all involved
Our Angel customers in the UK, US and Australia have direct access to over 300 world-class independent winemakers and over 2,500 quality wines from 23 countries.
For more information visit
nakedwinesplc.co.uk
and
nakedwines.co.uk
or find us
@nakedwines

is not just an online wine retailer; we're trailblazers on a mission to enable enthusiastic wine drinkers to enjoy great wine without the guesswork.Founded in 2008, on the pillars of quality, choice and fair pricing, we set out to create the most inclusive wine club in the world - dedicated to transforming the wine-buying experience and empowering people to make their own wine choices, and championing world-class independent winemakers. We've proudly been delivering outstanding wines to our customers (who we call Angels) for over 16 years.
Our business model is simple yet innovative: Naked Wines funds the production costs for winemakers upfront, allowing them to focus on creating exceptional wines without the financial burdens of traditional wine production, while passing the resulting savings back to our customers.
The virtuous circle is a win-win for both wine lovers and winemakers, and enables us to deliver superior benefits to our customers:
- Better quality wine
- More choice
- Personalised wine recommendations
- Elimination of guesswork and uncertainty
- Fair payments for all involved
Our Angel customers in the UK, US and Australia have direct access to over 300 world-class independent winemakers and over 2,500 quality wines from 23 countries.
For more information visit
nakedwinesplc.co.uk
and
nakedwines.co.uk
or find us
@nakedwines

latest.title: Director/PDMR Shareholding
latest.content:
28 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 9556F
Naked Wines PLC
28 May 2026
28 May 2026
Naked Wines Plc
(the "Company")
Director/PDMR Dealing
The Board of Naked Wines announces that Diane Pailing, a person closely associated with Jack Pailing, Non-Executive Chair of the Company, has today purchased 26,500 Ordinary Shares of 7.5 pence each in the Company ("Ordinary Shares"), and Nigel Pailing, also a person closely associated with Jack Pailing, purchased 26,250 Ordinary Shares.
Following the purchase of shares, Mr. Pailing's beneficial interest in the Company and that of persons closely associated with him is 1,064,593 Ordinary Shares representing approximately 1.59% of the total voting rights of the Company.

company_30s.what_they_do: WINE.L is a player in the wine market, likely involved in the retail or distribution of alcoholic beverages. The company’s core business revolves around sourcing, selling, and possibly marketing wine to consumers or other businesses.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Wine is a global, relatively resilient consumer market with steady demand across multiple regions. A firm that can capture even a modest share of that market can generate stable cash flows, especially if it has a unique distribution network or brand partnerships.
company_30s.current_state: The company has been trading in a very low range, indicating a prolonged period of underperformance. Management is actively engaging in share buybacks and has recently altered insider holdings, suggesting an attempt to support the stock. The shares are trading at a steep discount relative to their historic valuation, implying a significant margin for upside if fundamentals improve.

rns_narrative: Management is actively managing its equity base: directors are adjusting their shareholdings, the company is buying back its own stock, and it has been filing block‑trade updates. These moves point to an effort to tighten liquidity and possibly signal confidence in the business.

The company’s most recent filings also include updates on its own share transactions and holdings, indicating that insiders are still involved in the company’s capital structure. This can be interpreted as a sign that those with inside knowledge believe the stock is undervalued.

Overall, the narrative is one of active capital management rather than a change in core business strategy. There is no evidence of a new product launch or regulatory win, but the buyback program may act as a catalyst if it is completed.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism with a heavy dose of skepticism
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Buybacks suggest insider confidence, but the market remains wary because there is no new growth story.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Buybacks are a waste of capital; the price has not yet shown any signs of reversal and could stay flat or drop further.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter is mostly static—no new headlines are sparking enthusiasm, but there is also no active negative pressure. The mood is one of patient waiting.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, meaning the broader public isn’t looking for this stock. That gives contrarian traders a window for surprise when a catalyst finally appears.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the announced share‑buyback program, which could lift the stock if the market interprets it as a vote of confidence.
A positive earnings report that beats expectations, especially if it includes guidance for a turnaround.
A volume spike at the 50‑day or 200‑day moving‑average support level, indicating institutional buying.
Any regulatory or partnership announcement that expands the company’s distribution network or introduces a new product line.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained volume increase (at least 3‑4 times the 10‑day average) coupled with a price bounce off the 200‑day moving average. Also monitor insider buying; a surge in director purchases can be a strong confirmation.
expect_next.timing_regime: The shares are deep in a crash, likely past the initial capitulation phase. The APEX score (not shown) would confirm a tight compression, which is a classic sign that a mean‑reversion bounce could be imminent once volume starts to pick up.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is down about 86% from its peak. If it rebounds to the halfway point of that decline, the upside is roughly 300% of the current price. Historical RED and PURPLE signals have delivered massive gains, and deep crashes often produce the most spectacular recoveries. The buyback program could act as a catalyst that triggers the mean‑reversion.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company has not demonstrated any new growth engine or improved earnings. If the buyback stalls or the market continues to ignore the stock, the price could stay flat or fall to zero. The worst case is a total collapse, wiping out the remaining equity.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: ~300% (current price to halfway recovery). Potential downside: 100% (price to zero). Risk‑reward is roughly 1:3, which is favorable for a contrarian strategy that tolerates a full loss for a large payoff.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a clear bounce off the 200‑day moving‑average support, with a stop 20% below that level to protect against a false breakout. Size the position at 5‑10% of your trading capital, taking advantage of the large upside potential while limiting downside exposure.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a 5‑fold volume spike on a day when the price closes above the 200‑day moving average. That would be a strong confirmation that the market is ready to resume buying.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk has been identified. The stock has not shown any signs of delisting, bankruptcy, or fraud. Therefore, the default stance remains bullish if the mean‑reversion plays out.

ai_insights.bottom_line: With an 86% crash and a buyback program as the only near‑term catalyst, WINE.L offers a contrarian opportunity: a 300% upside if it recovers halfway, but a full loss if it fails to bounce.
